premise: When my sister died, I moved in at 49 to raise her two kids alongside my brother-in-law, sinking $60,000 of my own savings into the house and their college funds, only for him to remarry a woman who convinced him I was just a freeloading tenant — they evicted me via a fake lease and diverted a $200,000 life insurance check my sister had left for the children into their own account. I lawyered up, proved a constructive trust, recovered every cent into a court-supervised trust with me as trustee, then sued for reimbursement and forced the sale of his house; I used the $310,000 settlement to buy a cottage on the property next door and now host the kids every holiday while he rents a basement apartment.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Brother-In-Law Evicted Me And Stole $200K From His Kids. So I Watched Him Los
